questions.q: How did Dhrishtadyumna come into the world?
options: Born to Drupada's queen after long penance
Found floating in a basket on the Ganga
Carved from clay and given life by Shiva
Stepping full-grown from a sacrificial fire, armed and armored
questions.explanation: Dhrishtadyumna emerged from Drupada's sacrificial fire already grown, wearing armor and bearing weapons.

questions.q: What single destiny was Dhrishtadyumna born to fulfill?
questions.explanation: He was born from the rite Drupada commissioned for one purpose: a son who would slay Drona.

questions.q: Who was Dhrishtadyumna's famous sister?
questions.explanation: Draupadi rose from the same sacrificial fire moments after him, making them fire-born siblings.

questions.q: What position did Dhrishtadyumna hold in the Kurukshetra war?
options: Supreme commander of the Pandava armies
questions.explanation: Dhrishtadyumna served as senapati of the Pandava host for all eighteen days of the war.

questions.q: Who was Dhrishtadyumna's father?
questions.explanation: He was born from the sacrificial fire of Drupada, king of Panchala, as the instrument of his father's revenge.

questions.q: Which great teacher did Dhrishtadyumna behead at Kurukshetra?
questions.explanation: Dhrishtadyumna cut off the head of Drona, the weapons master of the Kuru princes, fulfilling his birth prophecy.

questions.q: Of which kingdom was Dhrishtadyumna a prince?
questions.explanation: As Drupada's son he was a prince of Panchala, the proud realm with its capital at Kampilya.

questions.q: How did Dhrishtadyumna die?
options: Felled by arrows on the final day of battle
In a dawn duel with Ashwatthama
Attacked while asleep in the night raid on the camp
By a curse pronounced by the sage Durvasa
questions.explanation: He was attacked in his sleep and killed when the Pandava camp was butchered on the night after the war's eighteenth day.

questions.q: Who killed Dhrishtadyumna?
questions.explanation: Ashwatthama killed him in the night raid as revenge for the beheading of his father Drona.

questions.q: Which of Dhrishtadyumna's siblings was the reborn princess Amba?
questions.explanation: Shikhandi, Drupada's child raised as a son, was Amba reborn to bring about Bhishma's fall.

questions.q: Who trained Dhrishtadyumna in the science of arms?
questions.explanation: Knowing the boy was born to kill him, Drona still accepted him as a pupil, holding that fate could not be evaded.

questions.q: What role did Dhrishtadyumna perform at Draupadi's swayamvara?
options: He guarded the great bow from theft
He competed to set the standard for suitors
He announced the contest and presented the assembled suitors
He strung the bow for each challenger
questions.explanation: As Draupadi's brother he proclaimed the terms of the bow-and-target contest and named the suitors to his sister.

questions.q: What did Dhrishtadyumna do after a disguised brahmin won his sister?
options: He challenged the stranger to a duel
He rode to inform Krishna at Dwarka
He demanded the contest be annulled
He secretly followed the brothers to their lodging to learn who they were
questions.explanation: He trailed the victors to the potter's house and overheard their warlike talk, then assured Drupada they were kshatriyas.

questions.q: In what state was Drona when Dhrishtadyumna beheaded him?
options: Seated in meditation, having laid down his weapons
Wounded and pinned beneath his chariot
Asleep in his tent at midday
questions.explanation: Believing his son dead, Drona laid down his arms and sat in yoga on his chariot, and Dhrishtadyumna struck off his head.

questions.q: Which Pandava-side warrior nearly came to blows with Dhrishtadyumna over the manner of Drona's killing?
questions.explanation: Satyaki denounced the slaying of a weaponless guru, and the two had to be physically separated by their comrades.

questions.q: What news broke Drona's will to fight shortly before Dhrishtadyumna struck?
options: Word that Duryodhana had fled the field
News that Karna had fallen to Arjuna
The report of Ashwatthama's death, confirmed by Yudhishthira
Tidings that Hastinapura had been sacked
questions.explanation: Bhima had killed an elephant named Ashwatthama, and Yudhishthira's half-true confirmation drained Drona of the will to fight.

questions.q: Of which deity was Dhrishtadyumna considered a partial incarnation?
questions.explanation: The epic's list of divine descents names the fire-born Dhrishtadyumna as a portion of Agni, the fire god.

questions.q: Why was Dhrishtadyumna's death considered a denial of warrior's honor?
options: He was beaten and smothered without a weapon, despite begging to die by the sword
He was poisoned at his evening meal
He was killed while fleeing the battlefield
He was executed in chains before the army
questions.explanation: Ashwatthama deliberately refused him death by a blade, killing him bare-handed so he would not earn a warrior's heaven.

questions.q: Whose humiliation was Dhrishtadyumna born to avenge?
options: His father Drupada's defeat and loss of half Panchala to Drona
Draupadi's insult in the dice hall
The Panchala losses at the Rajasuya conquests
questions.explanation: Drona had Drupada captured and took half his kingdom, and Drupada's burning shame produced the fire-born avenger.

questions.q: Which two allied kings did Drona slay on the same day Dhrishtadyumna finally killed him?
questions.explanation: On the fifteenth day Drona killed both Drupada and Virata, deaths that further enraged Dhrishtadyumna before the beheading.

questions.q: What does the name Dhrishtadyumna roughly signify?
options: One of bold and radiant splendor
questions.explanation: The name joins dhrishta, meaning bold or daring, with dyumna, meaning splendor or glory.

questions.q: Which Pandava first proposed Dhrishtadyumna as supreme commander at the war council?
questions.explanation: When Yudhishthira polled his brothers, Arjuna named Dhrishtadyumna, and Krishna endorsed the choice.

questions.q: What event set Drupada on the path that led to Dhrishtadyumna's fire-birth?
options: The burning of Kampilya by Kuru raiders
His defeat by Drona's students and the loss of half his kingdom
An insult from Bhishma at a royal assembly
A prophecy delivered at Draupadi's naming
questions.explanation: Captured by Arjuna as Drona's teaching fee and stripped of northern Panchala, Drupada sought a son who could kill Drona.

questions.q: Which sages performed the rite from which Dhrishtadyumna was born?
questions.explanation: The brahmin brothers Yaja and Upayaja conducted Drupada's sacrifice, and the armed youth rose from its flames.

questions.q: How exactly did Ashwatthama kill Dhrishtadyumna in the night raid?
options: He cut his throat with Drona's own sword
He shot him with arrows as he reached for his bow
He set the commander's tent ablaze around him
He throttled and pounded him to death, refusing his plea to die by a blade
questions.explanation: Ashwatthama woke him with kicks and beat and strangled him, deliberately denying the sword death Dhrishtadyumna begged for.

questions.q: What had Arjuna urged regarding Drona just before Dhrishtadyumna struck the fatal blow?
options: That the guru be taken alive rather than slain
That Drona be left for him alone to face
That Drona be allowed to retire from the field
That the army withdraw rather than fight him
questions.explanation: Arjuna shouted for Drona to be captured alive, but Dhrishtadyumna beheaded the seated guru as the army watched.

questions.q: On which day of the eighteen-day war did Dhrishtadyumna kill Drona?
questions.explanation: Drona fell on the fifteenth day, after the false report of his son's death broke his spirit.

questions.q: Drona vowed to capture which Pandava alive, a plan Dhrishtadyumna's forces constantly thwarted?
questions.explanation: Drona promised Duryodhana he would seize Yudhishthira alive, and Dhrishtadyumna stood as the king's sworn shield against him.

questions.q: When Dhrishtadyumna arrayed the Pandava host on the first morning of the war, who commanded the opposing army?
questions.explanation: Bhishma led the Kaurava forces for the first ten days, opposite the fire-born commander of the Pandavas.

questions.q: Who held the camp gates while Ashwatthama slaughtered Dhrishtadyumna and the sleeping Panchalas?
questions.explanation: Kripa and Kritavarma guarded the exits and set fires, cutting down any who fled the camp as Ashwatthama killed within.
